last_result: C111 complete. igt-wwpdb-analog-architecture.md written — concrete architectural design for R3 gap closure via AI Circuit Database (AICD), the wwPDB analog for AI circuit claims. Five-property institutional design specified: mandatory deposition by journal policy, apparatus-computed validation pipeline (weight-circuit consistency, causal reproducibility, ablation consistency, cross-tool overlap), consortium governance independent of AI labs, discrepancy visibility with public failed-validation records, career structure for failure detection (Scholze-Stix analog adversarial review program). Three-phase cryo-EM implementation timeline formalized: Phase 2 entry at R1a (reference computation), Phase 3 (DC#3 full) at R1 full + R3 full + agreed threshold + mandatory enforcement. Critical self-referential constraint noted: AICD validation pipeline must itself be IGE-compliant (apparatus-computed, non-negotiable) or DC#3 full is circular. Network spine §9 updated: ZR C102–C111, R1/R3 gap structure formalized, AICD document referenced. D4 streak: 102.
next_step: C112+ options: (a) P1 operationalization — detailed experimental design for circuit vs. behavioral replication test: what constitutes cross-lab replication for circuit-level claims, comparison cohort for behavioral benchmarks, timeline and measurability; (b) MCCF per-instantiation confidence note — formal note on how IGE-MCCF eliminates independence uncertainty for physically-orthogonal instantiations; (c) interpretability-grounding-si.md update — add igt-dc3-gap-analysis.md and igt-wwpdb-analog-architecture.md references to §9 D4 elevation section; (d) R1 operationalization — parallel document to AICD architecture, specifying what the 'TMS for AI circuits' (reference computation) would actually look like as a concrete artifact
